001/*
002 * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more
003 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with
004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.rest.server;
018
019import static java.lang.annotation.ElementType.*;
020import static java.lang.annotation.RetentionPolicy.*;
021
022import java.lang.annotation.*;
023
024import org.apache.juneau.bean.swagger.*;
025import org.apache.juneau.commons.*;
026import org.apache.juneau.commons.inject.*;
027import org.apache.juneau.http.remote.*;
028import org.apache.juneau.marshall.encoders.*;
029import org.apache.juneau.marshall.parser.*;
030import org.apache.juneau.marshall.serializer.*;
031import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.converter.*;
032import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.guard.*;
033import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.matcher.*;
034import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.metrics.*;
035import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.servlet.*;
036import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.swagger.*;
037
038/**
039 * Identifies a REST operation Java method on a {@link RestServlet} implementation class.
040 *
041 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
042 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestOpAnnotatedMethodBasics">@RestOp-Annotated Method Basics</a>
043
044 * </ul>
045 */
046@Target(METHOD)
047@Retention(RUNTIME)
048@Inherited
049@AnnotationGroup(RestOp.class)
050public @interface RestOp {
051
052   /**
053    * Specifies whether this method can be called based on the client version.
054    *
055    * <p>
056    * The client version is identified via the HTTP request header identified by
057    * {@link Rest#clientVersionHeader() @Rest(clientVersionHeader)} which by default is <js>"Client-Version"</js>.
058    *
059    * <p>
060    * This is a specialized kind of {@link RestMatcher} that allows you to invoke different Java methods for the same
061    * method/path based on the client version.
062    *
063    * <p>
064    * The format of the client version range is similar to that of OSGi versions.
065    *
066    * <p>
067    * In the following example, the Java methods are mapped to the same HTTP method and URL <js>"/foobar"</js>.
068    * <p class='bjava'>
069    *    <jc>// Call this method if Client-Version is at least 2.0.
070    *    // Note that this also matches 2.0.1.</jc>
071    *    <ja>@RestOp</ja>(method=<jsf>GET</jsf>, path=<js>"/foobar"</js>, clientVersion=<js>"2.0"</js>)
072    *    <jk>public</jk> Object method1()  {...}
073    *
074    *    <jc>// Call this method if Client-Version is at least 1.1, but less than 2.0.</jc>
075    *    <ja>@RestOp</ja>(method=<jsf>GET</jsf>, path=<js>"/foobar"</js>, clientVersion=<js>"[1.1,2.0)"</js>)
076    *    <jk>public</jk> Object method2()  {...}
077    *
078    *    <jc>// Call this method if Client-Version is less than 1.1.</jc>
079    *    <ja>@RestOp</ja>(method=<jsf>GET</jsf>, path=<js>"/foobar"</js>, clientVersion=<js>"[0,1.1)"</js>)
080    *    <jk>public</jk> Object method3()  {...}
081    * </p>
082    *
083    * <p>
084    * It's common to combine the client version with transforms that will convert new POJOs into older POJOs for
085    * backwards compatibility.
086    * <p class='bjava'>
087    *    <jc>// Call this method if Client-Version is at least 2.0.</jc>
088    *    <ja>@RestOp</ja>(method=<jsf>GET</jsf>, path=<js>"/foobar"</js>, clientVersion=<js>"2.0"</js>)
089    *    <jk>public</jk> NewPojo newMethod()  {...}
090    *
091    *    <jc>// Call this method if X-Client-Version is at least 1.1, but less than 2.0.</jc>
092    *    <ja>@RestOp</ja>(method=<jsf>GET</jsf>, path=<js>"/foobar"</js>, clientVersion=<js>"[1.1,2.0)"</js>)
093    *    <ja>@MarshalledConfig(swaps=NewToOldSwap.<jk>class</jk>)
094    *    <jk>public</jk> NewPojo oldMethod() {
095    *       <jk>return</jk> newMethod();
096    *    }
097    *
098    * <p>
099    * Note that in the previous example, we're returning the exact same POJO, but using a transform to convert it into
100    * an older form.
101    * The old method could also just return back a completely different object.
102    * The range can be any of the following:
103    * <ul>
104    *    <li><js>"[0,1.0)"</js> = Less than 1.0.  1.0 and 1.0.0 does not match.
105    *    <li><js>"[0,1.0]"</js> = Less than or equal to 1.0.  Note that 1.0.1 will match.
106    *    <li><js>"1.0"</js> = At least 1.0.  1.0 and 2.0 will match.
107    * </ul>
108    *
109    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
110    *    <li class='ja'>{@link Rest#clientVersionHeader()}
111    * </ul>
112    *
113    * @return The annotation value.
114    */
115   String clientVersion() default "";
116
117   /**
118    * Supported content media types.
119    *
120    * <p>
121    * Overrides the media types inferred from the parsers that identify what media types can be consumed by the resource.
122    *
123    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
124    *    <li class='note'>
125    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
126    *       (e.g. <js>"$S{mySystemProperty}"</js>).
127    * </ul>
128    *
129    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
130    *    <li class='jm'>{@link org.apache.juneau.rest.server.RestOpContext#getSupportedContentTypes()}
131    * </ul>
132    *
133    * @return The annotation value.
134    */
135   String[] consumes() default {};
136
137   /**
138    * Class-level response converters.
139    *
140    * <p>
141    * Associates one or more {@link RestConverter converters} with this method.
142    *
143    * @return The annotation value.
144    */
145   Class<? extends RestConverter>[] converters() default {};
146
147   /**
148    * Enable debug mode.
149    *
150    * <p>
151    * Enables the following:
152    * <ul class='spaced-list'>
153    *    <li>
154    *       HTTP request/response bodies are cached in memory for logging purposes.
155    *    <li>
156    *       Request/response messages are automatically logged.
157    * </ul>
158    *
159    * <ul class='values'>
160    *    <li><js>"true"</js> - Debug is enabled for all requests.
161    *    <li><js>"false"</js> - Debug is disabled for all requests.
162    *    <li><js>"conditional"</js> - Debug is enabled only for requests that have a <c class='snippet'>Debug: true</c> header.
163    *    <li><js>""</js> (or anything else) - Debug mode is inherited from class.
164    * </ul>
165    *
166    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
167    *    <li class='note'>
168    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
169    *       (e.g. <js>"$L{my.localized.variable}"</js>).
170    * </ul>
171    *
172    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
173    *    <li class='jm'>{@link org.apache.juneau.rest.server.RestContext#getDebugEnablement()}
174    * </ul>
175    *
176    * @return The annotation value.
177    */
178   Debug debug() default @Debug;
179
180   /**
181    * Default <c>Accept</c> header.
182    *
183    * <p>
184    * The default value for the <c>Accept</c> header if not specified on a request.
185    *
186    * <p>
187    * This is a shortcut for using {@link #defaultRequestHeaders()} for just this specific header.
188    *
189    * @return The annotation value.
190    */
191   String defaultAccept() default "";
192
193   /**
194    * Default character encoding.
195    *
196    * <p>
197    * The default character encoding for the request and response if not specified on the request.
198    *
199    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
200    *    <li class='note'>
201    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
202    *       (e.g. <js>"$S{mySystemProperty}"</js>).
203    * </ul>
204    *
205    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
206    *    <li class='ja'>{@link Rest#defaultCharset}
207    * </ul>
208    *
209    * @return The annotation value.
210    */
211   String defaultCharset() default "";
212
213   /**
214    * Default <c>Content-Type</c> header.
215    *
216    * <p>
217    * The default value for the <c>Content-Type</c> header if not specified on a request.
218    *
219    * <p>
220    * This is a shortcut for using {@link #defaultRequestHeaders()} for just this specific header.
221    *
222    * @return The annotation value.
223    */
224   String defaultContentType() default "";
225
226   /**
227    * Default request attributes.
228    *
229    * <p>
230    * Specifies default values for request attributes if they're not already set on the request.
231    *
232    * <p>
233    * Affects values returned by the following methods:
234    *    <ul>
235    *       <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#getAttribute(String)}.
236    *       <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#getAttributes()}.
237    *    </ul>
238    *
239    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
240    * <p class='bjava'>
241    *    <jc>// Defined via annotation resolving to a config file setting with default value.</jc>
242    *    <ja>@Rest</ja>(defaultRequestAttributes={<js>"Foo=bar"</js>, <js>"Baz: $C{REST/myAttributeValue}"</js>})
243    *    <jk>public class</jk> MyResource {
244    *
245    *       <jc>// Override at the method level.</jc>
246    *       <ja>@RestGet</ja>(defaultRequestAttributes={<js>"Foo: bar"</js>})
247    *       <jk>public</jk> Object myMethod() {...}
248    *    }
249    * </p>
250    *
251    * </ul>
252    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
253    *    <li class='note'>
254    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
255    *       (e.g. <js>"$L{my.localized.variable}"</js>).
256    * </ul>
257    *
258    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
259    *    <li class='ja'>{@link Rest#defaultRequestAttributes()}
260    * </ul>
261    *
262    * @return The annotation value.
263    */
264   String[] defaultRequestAttributes() default {};
265
266   /**
267    * Specifies default values for form-data parameters.
268    *
269    * <p>
270    * Strings are of the format <js>"name=value"</js>.
271    *
272    * <p>
273    * Affects values returned by {@link RestRequest#getFormParam(String)} when the parameter is not present on the
274    * request.
275    *
276    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
277    * <p class='bjava'>
278    *    <ja>@RestOp</ja>(method=<jsf>POST</jsf>, path=<js>"/*"</js>, defaultRequestFormData={<js>"foo=bar"</js>})
279    *    <jk>public</jk> String doPost(<ja>@FormData</ja>(<js>"foo"</js>) String <jv>foo</jv>)  {...}
280    * </p>
281    *
282    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
283    *    <li class='note'>
284    *       You can use either <js>':'</js> or <js>'='</js> as the key/value delimiter.
285    *    <li class='note'>
286    *       Key and value is trimmed of whitespace.
287    *    <li class='note'>
288    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
289    *       (e.g. <js>"$S{mySystemProperty}"</js>).
290    * </ul>
291    *
292    * @return The annotation value.
293    */
294   String[] defaultRequestFormData() default {};
295
296   /**
297    * Default request headers.
298    *
299    * <p>
300    * Specifies default values for request headers if they're not passed in through the request.
301    *
302    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
303    * <p class='bjava'>
304    *    <jc>// Assume "text/json" Accept value when Accept not specified</jc>
305    *    <ja>@RestOp</ja>(method=<jsf>GET</jsf>, path=<js>"/*"</js>, defaultRequestHeaders={<js>"Accept: text/json"</js>})
306    *    <jk>public</jk> String doGet()  {...}
307    * </p>
308    *
309    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
310    *    <li class='note'>
311    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
312    *       (e.g. <js>"$S{mySystemProperty}"</js>).
313    * </ul>
314    *
315    * @return The annotation value.
316    */
317   String[] defaultRequestHeaders() default {};
318
319   /**
320    * Specifies default values for query parameters.
321    *
322    * <p>
323    * Strings are of the format <js>"name=value"</js>.
324    *
325    * <p>
326    * Affects values returned by {@link RestRequest#getQueryParam(String)} when the parameter is not present on the request.
327    *
328    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
329    * <p class='bjava'>
330    *    <ja>@RestOp</ja>(method=<jsf>GET</jsf>, path=<js>"/*"</js>, defaultRequestQueryData={<js>"foo=bar"</js>})
331    *    <jk>public</jk> String doGet(<ja>@Query</ja>(<js>"foo"</js>) String <jv>foo</jv>)  {...}
332    * </p>
333    *
334    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
335    *    <li class='note'>
336    *       You can use either <js>':'</js> or <js>'='</js> as the key/value delimiter.
337    *    <li class='note'>
338    *       Key and value is trimmed of whitespace.
339    *    <li class='note'>
340    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
341    *       (e.g. <js>"$S{mySystemProperty}"</js>).
342    * </ul>
343    *
344    * @return The annotation value.
345    */
346   String[] defaultRequestQueryData() default {};
347
348   /**
349    * Default response headers.
350    *
351    * <p>
352    * Specifies default values for response headers if they're not overwritten during the request.
353    *
354    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
355    * <p class='bjava'>
356    *    <jc>// Assume "text/json" Accept value when Accept not specified</jc>
357    *    <ja>@RestOp</ja>(method=<jsf>GET</jsf>, path=<js>"/*"</js>, defaultResponseHeaders={<js>"Content-Type: text/json"</js>})
358    *    <jk>public</jk> String doGet()  {...}
359    * </p>
360    *
361    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
362    *    <li class='note'>
363    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
364    *       (e.g. <js>"$S{mySystemProperty}"</js>).
365    * </ul>
366    *
367    * @return The annotation value.
368    */
369   String[] defaultResponseHeaders() default {};
370
371   /**
372    * Optional description for the exposed API.
373    *
374    * <p>
375    * This description is used in the following locations:
376    * <ul class='spaced-list'>
377    *    <li>
378    *       The value returned by {@link Operation#getDescription()} in the auto-generated swagger.
379    *    <li>
380    *       The <js>"$RS{operationDescription}"</js> variable.
381    *    <li>
382    *       The description of the method in the Swagger page.
383    * </ul>
384    *
385    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
386    *    <li class='note'>
387    *       Corresponds to the swagger field <c>/paths/{path}/{method}/description</c>.
388    *    <li class='note'>
389    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
390    *       (e.g. <js>"$L{my.localized.variable}"</js>).
391    * </ul>
392    *
393    * @return The annotation value.
394    */
395   String[] description() default {};
396
397   /**
398    * Specifies the compression encoders for this method.
399    *
400    * <p>
401    * Encoders are used to enable various kinds of compression (e.g. <js>"gzip"</js>) on requests and responses.
402    *
403    * <p>
404    * This value overrides encoders specified at the class level using {@link Rest#encoders()}.
405    * The {@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.encoders.EncoderSet.Inherit} class can be used to include values from the parent class.
406    *
407    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
408    * <p class='bjava'>
409    *    <jc>// Define a REST resource that handles GZIP compression.</jc>
410    *    <ja>@Rest</ja>(
411    *       encoders={
412    *          GzipEncoder.<jk>class</jk>
413    *       }
414    *    )
415    *    <jk>public class</jk> MyResource {
416    *
417    *       <jc>// Define a REST method that can also use a custom encoder.</jc>
418    *       <ja>@RestOp</ja>(
419    *          method=<jsf>GET</jsf>,
420    *          encoders={
421    *             EncoderSet.Inherit.<jk>class</jk>, MyEncoder.<jk>class</jk>
422    *          }
423    *       )
424    *       <jk>public</jk> MyBean doGet() {
425    *          ...
426    *       }
427    *    }
428    * </p>
429    *
430    * <p>
431    * For programmatic equivalents, contribute a {@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.encoders.EncoderSet} bean via
432    * {@link Bean @Bean(name="encoders")} (use methodScope to scope to specific operation methods).
433    *
434    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
435    *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerEncoders">Encoders</a>
436    * </ul>
437    *
438    * @return The annotation value.
439    */
440   Class<? extends Encoder>[] encoders() default {};
441
442   /**
443    * Method-level guards.
444    *
445    * <p>
446    * Associates one or more {@link RestGuard RestGuards} with this method.
447    *
448    * @return The annotation value.
449    */
450   Class<? extends RestGuard>[] guards() default {};
451
452   /**
453    * Method matchers.
454    *
455    * <p>
456    * Associates one more more {@link RestMatcher RestMatchers} with this method.
457    *
458    * <p>
459    * Matchers are used to allow multiple Java methods to handle requests assigned to the same URL path pattern, but
460    * differing based on some request attribute, such as a specific header value.
461    *
462    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
463    *    <li class='jac'>{@link RestMatcher}
464    * </ul>
465    *
466    * @return The annotation value.
467    */
468   Class<? extends RestMatcher>[] matchers() default {};
469
470   /**
471    * The maximum allowed input size (in bytes) on HTTP requests.
472    *
473    * <p>
474    * Useful for alleviating DoS attacks by throwing an exception when too much input is received instead of resulting
475    * in out-of-memory errors which could affect system stability.
476    *
477    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
478    * <p class='bjava'>
479    *    <ja>@RestOp</ja>(
480    *       maxInput=<js>"100M"</js>
481    *    )
482    * </p>
483    *
484    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
485    *    <li>
486    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
487    *       (e.g. <js>"$S{mySystemProperty}"</js>).
488    * </ul>
489    *
490    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
491    *    <li class='ja'>{@link Rest#maxInput}
492    * </ul>
493    *
494    * @return The annotation value.
495    */
496   String maxInput() default "";
497
498   /**
499    * REST method name.
500    *
501    * <p>
502    * Typically <js>"GET"</js>, <js>"PUT"</js>, <js>"POST"</js>, <js>"DELETE"</js>, or <js>"OPTIONS"</js>.
503    *
504    * <p>
505    * Method names are case-insensitive (always folded to upper-case).
506    *
507    * <p>
508    * Note that you can use {@link org.apache.juneau.http.HttpMethod} for constant values.
509    *
510    * <p>
511    * Note that you can also use {@link #value()} to specify the method name and path in shortened form.
512    *
513    * <p>
514    * Besides the standard HTTP method names, the following can also be specified:
515    * <ul class='spaced-list'>
516    *    <li>
517    *       <js>"*"</js>
518    *       - Denotes any method.
519    *       <br>Use this if you want to capture any HTTP methods in a single Java method.
520    *       <br>The {@link Method @Method} annotation and/or {@link RestRequest#getMethod()} method can be used to
521    *       distinguish the actual HTTP method name.
522    *    <li>
523    *       <js>""</js>
524    *       - Auto-detect.
525    *       <br>The method name is determined based on the Java method name.
526    *       <br>For example, if the method is <c>doPost(...)</c>, then the method name is automatically detected
527    *       as <js>"POST"</js>.
528    *       <br>Otherwise, defaults to <js>"GET"</js>.
529    *    <li>
530    *       <js>"RRPC"</js>
531    *       - Remote-proxy interface.
532    *       <br>This denotes a Java method that returns an object (usually an interface, often annotated with the
533    *       {@link Remote @Remote} annotation) to be used as a remote proxy using
534    *       <c>RestClient.getRemoteInterface(Class&lt;T&gt; interfaceClass, String url)</c>.
535    *       <br>This allows you to construct client-side interface proxies using REST as a transport medium.
536    *       <br>Conceptually, this is simply a fancy <c>POST</c> against the url <js>"/{path}/{javaMethodName}"</js>
537    *       where the arguments are marshalled from the client to the server as an HTTP content containing an array of
538    *       objects, passed to the method as arguments, and then the resulting object is marshalled back to the client.
539    *    <li>
540    *       Anything else
541    *       - Overloaded non-HTTP-standard names that are passed in through a <c>&amp;method=methodName</c> URL
542    *       parameter.
543    * </ul>
544    *
545    * @return The annotation value.
546    */
547   String method() default "";
548
549   /**
550    * Specifies the parsers for converting HTTP request bodies into POJOs for this method.
551    *
552    * <p>
553    * Parsers are used to convert the content of HTTP requests into POJOs.
554    * <br>Any of the Juneau framework parsers can be used in this setting.
555    * <br>The parser selected is based on the request <c>Content-Type</c> header matched against the values returned by the following method
556    * using a best-match algorithm:
557    * <ul class='javatree'>
558    *    <li class='jm'>{@link Parser#getMediaTypes()}
559    * </ul>
560    *
561    * <p>
562    * This value overrides parsers specified at the class level using {@link Rest#parsers()}.
563    * The {@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.parser.ParserSet.Inherit} class can be used to include values from the parent class.
564    *
565    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
566    * <p class='bjava'>
567    *    <jc>// Define a REST resource that can consume JSON and HTML.</jc>
568    *    <ja>@Rest</ja>(
569    *       parsers={
570    *          JsonParser.<jk>class</jk>,
571    *          HtmlParser.<jk>class</jk>
572    *       }
573    *    )
574    *    <jk>public class</jk> MyResource {
575    *
576    *       <jc>// Define a REST method that can also consume XML.</jc>
577    *       <ja>@RestOp</ja>(
578    *          method=<jsf>POST</jsf>,
579    *          parsers={
580    *             ParserSet.Inherit.<jk>class</jk>, XmlParser.<jk>class</jk>
581    *          }
582    *       )
583    *       <jk>public void</jk> doPost(MyBean <jv>bean</jv>) {
584    *          ...
585    *       }
586    *    }
587    * </p>
588    *
589    * <p>
590    * For programmatic equivalents, contribute a {@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.parser.ParserSet} bean via
591    * {@link Bean @Bean(name="parsers")} (use methodScope to scope to specific operation methods).
592    *
593    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
594    *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/Marshalling">Marshalling</a>
595    * </ul>
596    *
597    * @return The annotation value.
598    */
599   Class<?>[] parsers() default {};
600
601   /**
602    * Optional path pattern for the specified method.
603    *
604    * <p>
605    * Appending <js>"/*"</js> to the end of the path pattern will make it match any remainder too.
606    * <br>Not appending <js>"/*"</js> to the end of the pattern will cause a 404 (Not found) error to occur if the exact
607    * pattern is not found.
608    *
609    * <p>
610    * The path can contain variables that get resolved to {@link org.apache.juneau.http.Path @Path} parameters.
611    *
612    * <h5 class='figure'>Examples:</h5>
613    * <p class='bjava'>
614    *    <ja>@RestOp</ja>(method=<jsf>GET</jsf>, path=<js>"/myurl/{foo}/{bar}/{baz}/*"</js>)
615    * </p>
616    * <p class='bjava'>
617    *    <ja>@RestOp</ja>(method=<jsf>GET</jsf>, path=<js>"/myurl/{0}/{1}/{2}/*"</js>)
618    * </p>
619    *
620    * <p>
621    * If you do not specify a path name, then the path name is inferred from the Java method name.
622    *
623    * <h5 class='figure'>Example:</h5>
624    * <p class='bjava'>
625    *    <jc>// Path is assumed to be "/foo".</jc>
626    *    <ja>@RestOp</ja>(method=<jsf>GET</jsf>)
627    *    <jk>public void</jk> foo() {...}
628    * </p>
629    *
630    * <p>
631    * If you also do not specify the {@link #method()} and the Java method name starts with <js>"get"</js>, <js>"put"</js>, <js>"post"</js>, or <js>"deleted"</js>,
632    * then the HTTP method name is stripped from the inferred path.
633    *
634    * <h5 class='figure'>Examples:</h5>
635    * <p class='bjava'>
636    *    <jc>// Method is GET, path is "/foo".</jc>
637    *    <ja>@RestOp</ja>
638    *    <jk>public void</jk> getFoo() {...}
639    * </p>
640    * <p class='bjava'>
641    *    <jc>// Method is DELETE, path is "/bar".</jc>
642    *    <ja>@RestOp</ja>
643    *    <jk>public void</jk> deleteBar() {...}
644    * </p>
645    * <p class='bjava'>
646    *    <jc>// Method is GET, path is "/foobar".</jc>
647    *    <ja>@RestOp</ja>
648    *    <jk>public void</jk> foobar() {...}
649    * </p>
650    * <p class='bjava'>
651    *    <jc>// Method is GET, path is "/".</jc>
652    *    <ja>@RestOp</ja>
653    *    <jk>public void</jk> get() {...}
654    * </p>
655    *
656    * <p>
657    * Note that you can also use {@link #value()} to specify the method name and path in shortened form.
658    *
659    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
660    *    <li class='ja'>{@link org.apache.juneau.http.Path}
661    * </ul>
662    *
663    * @return The annotation value.
664    */
665   String[] path() default {};
666
667   /**
668    * Per-operation override for {@link Rest#problemDetails() @Rest(problemDetails)}.
669    *
670    * <p>
671    * When set on a {@code @RestOp} method, this value takes precedence over the resource-level
672    * {@code @Rest(problemDetails)} attribute for this operation. Tri-state semantics:
673    *
674    * <ul class='values'>
675    *    <li><js>"true"</js> &mdash; enable problem-details responses for this operation.
676    *    <li><js>"false"</js> &mdash; disable problem-details responses for this operation (overrides an
677    *       opted-in resource).
678    *    <li><js>""</js> (default) &mdash; inherit from {@link Rest#problemDetails()}.
679    * </ul>
680    *
681    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
682    *    <li class='note'>
683    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
684    *       (e.g. <js>"$L{my.localized.variable}"</js>).
685    * </ul>
686    *
687    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
688    *    <li class='ja'>{@link Rest#problemDetails()}
689    * </ul>
690    *
691    * @return The annotation value.
692    */
693   String problemDetails() default "";
694
695   /**
696    * Per-operation override for {@link Rest#virtualThreads() @Rest(virtualThreads)}.
697    *
698    * <p>
699    * When set, this value takes precedence over the resource-level setting for this operation. Tri-state
700    * semantics:
701    *
702    * <ul class='values'>
703    *    <li><js>"true"</js> &mdash; enable virtual-thread dispatch for this operation (Java 21+ only;
704    *       gracefully ignored on older JVMs).
705    *    <li><js>"false"</js> &mdash; disable virtual-thread dispatch for this operation (overrides an
706    *       opted-in resource).
707    *    <li><js>""</js> (default) &mdash; inherit from {@link Rest#virtualThreads()}.
708    * </ul>
709    *
710    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
711    *    <li class='note'>
712    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
713    *       (e.g. <js>"$E{ENABLE_VT,false}"</js>).
714    * </ul>
715    *
716    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
717    *    <li class='ja'>{@link Rest#virtualThreads()}
718    * </ul>
719    *
720    * @return The annotation value.
721    */
722   String virtualThreads() default "";
723
724   /**
725    * Per-operation observability opt-in / opt-out control.
726    *
727    * <p>
728    * Overrides the resource-level {@link Rest#observability()} setting for this specific operation.
729    * Controls whether the observability block ({@link org.apache.juneau.rest.server.metrics.MetricsRecorder} /
730    * {@link org.apache.juneau.rest.server.tracing.TracerHook}) fires for this operation.
731    *
732    * <ul class='values'>
733    *    <li><js>"true"</js> &mdash; strict opt-in: this operation <em>requires</em> a wired observability backend.
734    *       If neither a {@code @Bean MetricsRecorder} nor a {@code @Bean TracerHook} is registered when the
735    *       {@link org.apache.juneau.rest.server.RestContext} is built, construction fails with a precise error.
736    *    <li><js>"false"</js> &mdash; explicit opt-out: the observability block is short-circuited for this operation,
737    *       even when a wired backend is present. Also suppresses
738    *       {@link org.apache.juneau.rest.server.tracing.TraceContextResponseProcessor} header injection.
739    *    <li><js>""</js> (default) &mdash; inherits the resource-level {@link Rest#observability()} setting.
740    *       If the resource-level setting is also empty, uses the existing behavior: the observability block
741    *       runs per-op, but a missing backend silently falls back to the NoOp singleton.
742    * </ul>
743    *
744    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
745    *    <li class='note'>
746    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
747    *       (e.g. <js>"$E{ENABLE_OP_OBSERVABILITY,false}"</js>).
748    *    <li class='note'>
749    *       This per-operation setting takes full precedence over {@link Rest#observability()}.
750    * </ul>
751    *
752    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
753    *    <li class='ja'>{@link Rest#observability()}
754    *    <li class='jc'>{@link org.apache.juneau.rest.server.metrics.MetricsRecorder}
755    *    <li class='jc'>{@link org.apache.juneau.rest.server.tracing.TracerHook}
756    * </ul>
757    *
758    * @return The annotation value.
759    * @since 10.0.0
760    */
761   String observability() default "";
762
763   /**
764    * Per-operation metric name override.
765    *
766    * <p>
767    * Overrides the metric name passed to {@link MetricsRecorder#record(String, String, String, int, java.time.Duration, Throwable, String, String) record()}
768    * for this operation.  When empty (default), the recorder uses its own default name derivation (typically the
769    * fully-qualified operation name).
770    *
771    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
772    *    <li class='note'>
773    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>.
774    *    <li class='note'>
775    *       Has no effect when observability is disabled for this operation.
776    * </ul>
777    *
778    * @return The annotation value.
779    * @since 10.0.0
780    */
781   String metricName() default "";
782
783   /**
784    * Per-operation metric tags.
785    *
786    * <p>
787    * Additional metric tags to pass to {@link MetricsRecorder#record(String, String, String, int, java.time.Duration, Throwable, String, String) record()}.
788    * Format: comma-separated {@code key=value} pairs, e.g. {@code "team=payments,region=us-east"}.
789    * When empty (default), no additional tags are passed.
790    *
791    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
792    *    <li class='note'>
793    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>.
794    *    <li class='note'>
795    *       The recorder implementation is responsible for parsing the comma-separated {@code key=value} string.
796    *    <li class='note'>
797    *       Has no effect when observability is disabled for this operation.
798    * </ul>
799    *
800    * @return The annotation value.
801    * @since 10.0.0
802    */
803   String metricTags() default "";
804
805   /**
806    * Per-operation override for {@link Rest#asyncTimeoutMillis() @Rest(asyncTimeoutMillis)}.
807    *
808    * <p>
809    * Configurable timeout (milliseconds) applied to {@link java.util.concurrent.CompletableFuture}-returning
810    * handlers. Default is 30,000 ms inherited from the resource. Set to {@code "0"} to disable the timeout.
811    *
812    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
813    *    <li class='note'>
814    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>.
815    * </ul>
816    *
817    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
818    *    <li class='ja'>{@link Rest#asyncTimeoutMillis()}
819    * </ul>
820    *
821    * @return The annotation value.
822    */
823   String asyncTimeoutMillis() default "";
824
825   /**
826    * Per-operation override for {@link Rest#asyncCompletionExecutor() @Rest(asyncCompletionExecutor)}.
827    *
828    * <p>
829    * Names the {@link java.util.concurrent.Executor} bean used to route completion callbacks for this
830    * specific operation through a dedicated thread pool. Empty string (default) inherits from
831    * {@link Rest#asyncCompletionExecutor()}.
832    *
833    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
834    *    <li class='ja'>{@link Rest#asyncCompletionExecutor()}
835    * </ul>
836    *
837    * @return The annotation value.
838    * @since 10.0.0
839    */
840   String asyncCompletionExecutor() default "";
841
842   /**
843    * Supported accept media types.
844    *
845    * <p>
846    * Overrides the media types inferred from the serializers that identify what media types can be produced by the resource.
847    *
848    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
849    *    <li class='note'>
850    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
851    *       (e.g. <js>"$S{mySystemProperty}"</js>).
852    * </ul>
853    *
854    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
855    *    <li class='jm'>{@link org.apache.juneau.rest.server.RestOpContext#getSupportedAcceptTypes()}
856    * </ul>
857    *
858    * @return The annotation value.
859    */
860   String[] produces() default {};
861
862   /**
863    * Allowed serializer session option keys for this operation (ordered merge, prefix {@code -key} removes a key).
864    *
865    * <p>
866    * Comma-delimited list of session property keys that clients may send via the <js>"X-Juneau-Serializer-Options"</js>
867    * header or <js>"juneauSerializerOptions"</js> query parameter.
868    * Merged on top of resource-level {@link Rest#allowedSerializerOptions()} values.
869    * Use {@link #noInherit()} to replace rather than extend the resource-level list.
870    *
871    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
872    *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/SessionOptions#safe-properties">Session Options - Safe Properties</a>
873    * </ul>
874    *
875    * @return The annotation value.
876    */
877   String[] allowedSerializerOptions() default {};
878
879   /**
880    * Allowed parser session option keys for this operation (ordered merge, prefix {@code -key} removes a key).
881    *
882    * <p>
883    * Comma-delimited list of session property keys that clients may send via the <js>"X-Juneau-Parser-Options"</js>
884    * header or <js>"juneauParserOptions"</js> query parameter.
885    * Merged on top of resource-level {@link Rest#allowedParserOptions()} values.
886    * Use {@link #noInherit()} to replace rather than extend the resource-level list.
887    *
888    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
889    *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/SessionOptions#safe-properties">Session Options - Safe Properties</a>
890    * </ul>
891    *
892    * @return The annotation value.
893    */
894   String[] allowedParserOptions() default {};
895
896   /**
897    * Property names for which less-derived contributions are NOT inherited.
898    *
899    * <p>
900    * Accepted values: {@code "allowedSerializerOptions"}, {@code "allowedParserOptions"}.
901    * Prevents the named property from inheriting values from the enclosing {@code @Rest} annotation.
902    * The {@code noInherit} attribute itself is never inherited.
903    *
904    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
905    *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/SessionOptions">Session Options</a>
906    * </ul>
907    *
908    * @return The annotation value.
909    */
910   String[] noInherit() default {};
911
912   /**
913    * Role guard.
914    *
915    * <p>
916    * An expression defining if a user with the specified roles are allowed to access this method.
917    *
918    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
919    * <p class='bjava'>
920    *    <jk>public class</jk> MyResource <jk>extends</jk> BasicRestServlet {
921    *
922    *       <ja>@RestOp</ja>(
923    *          method=<jsf>GET</jsf>,
924    *          path=<js>"/foo"</js>,
925    *          roleGuard=<js>"ROLE_ADMIN || (ROLE_READ_WRITE &amp;&amp; ROLE_SPECIAL)"</js>
926    *       )
927    *       <jk>public</jk> Object doGet() {
928    *       }
929    *    }
930    * </p>
931    *
932    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
933    *    <li class='note'>
934    *       Supports any of the following expression constructs:
935    *       <ul>
936    *          <li><js>"foo"</js> - Single arguments.
937    *          <li><js>"foo,bar,baz"</js> - Multiple OR'ed arguments.
938    *          <li><js>"foo | bar | baz"</js> - Multiple OR'ed arguments, pipe syntax.
939    *          <li><js>"foo || bar || baz"</js> - Multiple OR'ed arguments, Java-OR syntax.
940    *          <li><js>"fo*"</js> - Patterns including <js>'*'</js> and <js>'?'</js>.
941    *          <li><js>"fo* &amp; *oo"</js> - Multiple AND'ed arguments, ampersand syntax.
942    *          <li><js>"fo* &amp;&amp; *oo"</js> - Multiple AND'ed arguments, Java-AND syntax.
943    *          <li><js>"fo* || (*oo || bar)"</js> - Parenthesis.
944    *       </ul>
945    *    <li class='note'>
946    *       AND operations take precedence over OR operations (as expected).
947    *    <li class='note'>
948    *       Whitespace is ignored.
949    *    <li class='note'>
950    *       <jk>null</jk> or empty expressions always match as <jk>false</jk>.
951    *    <li class='note'>
952    *       If patterns are used, you must specify the list of declared roles using {@link #rolesDeclared()}.
953    *    <li class='note'>
954    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
955    *       (e.g. <js>"$L{my.localized.variable}"</js>).
956    *    <li class='note'>
957    *       When defined on parent/child classes and methods, ALL guards within the hierarchy must pass.
958    * </ul>
959    *
960    * @return The annotation value.
961    */
962   String roleGuard() default "";
963
964   /**
965    * Declared roles.
966    *
967    * <p>
968    * A comma-delimited list of all possible user roles.
969    *
970    * <p>
971    * Used in conjunction with {@link #roleGuard()} is used with patterns.
972    *
973    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
974    * <p class='bjava'>
975    *    <jk>public class</jk> MyResource <jk>extends</jk> BasicRestServlet {
976    *
977    *       <ja>@RestOp</ja>(
978    *          method=<jsf>GET</jsf>,
979    *          path=<js>"/foo"</js>,
980    *          rolesDeclared=<js>"ROLE_ADMIN,ROLE_READ_WRITE,ROLE_READ_ONLY,ROLE_SPECIAL"</js>,
981    *          roleGuard=<js>"ROLE_ADMIN || (ROLE_READ_WRITE &amp;&amp; ROLE_SPECIAL)"</js>
982    *       )
983    *       <jk>public</jk> Object doGet() {
984    *       }
985    *    }
986    * </p>
987    *
988    * @return The annotation value.
989    */
990   String rolesDeclared() default "";
991
992   /**
993    * Specifies the serializers for marshalling POJOs into response bodies for this method.
994    *
995    * <p>
996    * Serializer are used to convert POJOs to HTTP response bodies.
997    * <br>Any of the Juneau framework serializers can be used in this setting.
998    * <br>The serializer selected is based on the request <c>Accept</c> header matched against the values returned by the following method
999    * using a best-match algorithm:
1000    * <ul class='javatree'>
1001    *    <li class='jm'>{@link Serializer#getMediaTypeRanges()}
1002    * </ul>
1003    *
1004    * <p>
1005    * This value overrides serializers specified at the class level using {@link Rest#serializers()}.
1006    * The {@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.serializer.SerializerSet.Inherit} class can be used to include values from the parent class.
1007    *
1008    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1009    * <p class='bjava'>
1010    *    <jc>// Define a REST resource that can produce JSON and HTML.</jc>
1011    *    <ja>@Rest</ja>(
1012    *       serializers={
1013    *          JsonParser.<jk>class</jk>,
1014    *          HtmlParser.<jk>class</jk>
1015    *       }
1016    *    )
1017    *    <jk>public class</jk> MyResource {
1018    *
1019    *       <jc>// Define a REST method that can also produce XML.</jc>
1020    *       <ja>@RestOp</ja>(
1021    *          method=<jsf>POST</jsf>,
1022    *          parsers={
1023    *             SerializerSet.Inherit.<jk>class</jk>, XmlParser.<jk>class</jk>
1024    *          }
1025    *       )
1026    *       <jk>public void</jk> doPost(MyBean <jv>bean</jv>) {
1027    *          ...
1028    *       }
1029    *    }
1030    * </p>
1031    *
1032    * <p>
1033    * For programmatic equivalents, contribute a {@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.serializer.SerializerSet} bean via
1034    * {@link Bean @Bean(name="serializers")} (use methodScope to scope to specific operation methods).
1035    *
1036    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
1037    *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/Marshalling">Marshalling</a>
1038    * </ul>
1039    *
1040    * @return The annotation value.
1041    */
1042   Class<? extends Serializer>[] serializers() default {};
1043
1044   /**
1045    * Optional summary for the exposed API.
1046    *
1047    * <p>
1048    * This summary is used in the following locations:
1049    * <ul class='spaced-list'>
1050    *    <li>
1051    *       The value returned by {@link Operation#getSummary()} in the auto-generated swagger.
1052    *    <li>
1053    *       The <js>"$RS{operationSummary}"</js> variable.
1054    *    <li>
1055    *       The summary of the method in the Swagger page.
1056    * </ul>
1057    *
1058    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
1059    *    <li class='note'>
1060    *       Corresponds to the swagger field <c>/paths/{path}/{method}/summary</c>.
1061    *    <li class='note'>
1062    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
1063    *       (e.g. <js>"$L{my.localized.variable}"</js>).
1064    * </ul>
1065    *
1066    * @return The annotation value.
1067    */
1068   String summary() default "";
1069
1070   /**
1071    * Provides swagger-specific metadata on this method.
1072    *
1073    * <p>
1074    * Used to populate the auto-generated OPTIONS swagger documentation.
1075    *
1076    * <p>
1077    * The format of this annotation is JSON when all individual parts are concatenated.
1078    * <br>The starting and ending <js>'{'</js>/<js>'}'</js> characters around the entire value are optional.
1079    *
1080    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1081    * <p class='bjava'>
1082    *    <ja>@RestOp</ja>(
1083    *       method=<jsf>PUT</jsf>,
1084    *       path=<js>"/{propertyName}"</js>,
1085    *
1086    *       <jc>// Swagger info.</jc>
1087    *       swagger={
1088    *          <js>"parameters:["</js>,
1089    *             <js>"{name:'propertyName',in:'path',description:'The system property name.'},"</js>,
1090    *             <js>"{in:'body',description:'The new system property value.'}"</js>,
1091    *          <js>"],"</js>,
1092    *          <js>"responses:{"</js>,
1093    *             <js>"302: {headers:{Location:{description:'The root URL of this resource.'}}},"</js>,
1094    *             <js>"403: {description:'User is not an admin.'}"</js>,
1095    *          <js>"}"</js>
1096    *       }
1097    *    )
1098    * </p>
1099    *
1100    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
1101    *    <li class='note'>
1102    *       The format is <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/JuneauBeanSwagger2">juneau-bean-swagger-v2</a>.
1103    *       <br>Multiple lines are concatenated with newlines.
1104    *    <li class='note'>
1105    *       The starting and ending <js>'{'</js>/<js>'}'</js> characters around the entire value are optional.
1106    *    <li class='note'>
1107    *       These values are superimposed on top of any Swagger JSON file present for the resource in the classpath.
1108    *    <li class='note'>
1109    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
1110    *       (e.g. <js>"$L{my.localized.variable}"</js>).
1111    * </ul>
1112    *
1113    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
1114    *    <li class='ja'>{@link OpSwagger}
1115    *    <li class='jc'>{@link SwaggerProvider}
1116    * </ul>
1117    *
1118    * @return The annotation value.
1119    */
1120   OpSwagger swagger() default @OpSwagger;
1121
1122   /**
1123    * REST method name and path.
1124    *
1125    * <p>
1126    * Can be used to provide a shortened combined form for the {@link #method()} and {@link #path()} values.
1127    *
1128    * <p>
1129    * The following examples are considered equivalent.
1130    * <p class='bjava'>
1131    *    <jc>// Normal form</jc>
1132    *    <ja>@RestOp</ja>(method=<jsf>PUT</jsf>, path=<js>"/{propertyName}"</js>)
1133    *
1134    *    <jc>// Shortened form</jc>
1135    *    <ja>@RestOp</ja>(<js>"PUT /{propertyName}"</js>)
1136    * </p>
1137    *
1138    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
1139    *    <li class='note'>
1140    *       The path portion is optional.
1141    * </ul>
1142    *
1143    * @return The annotation value.
1144    */
1145   String value() default "";
1146}
